error_handling.overview: Error scenarios the UI Designer mode may encounter and the required responses. The most critical error scenario is test failure after visual changes — this triggers the revert-and-retry workflow.

error_scenario.name: test_failure_after_changes
error_scenario.description: One or more automated tests fail after visual changes are applied. This is the primary safety mechanism — it means changes crossed the visual/logic boundary.
error_scenario.detection: Run all automated tests after each batch of visual changes. Any non-zero exit code or test failure output triggers this scenario.

step.title: Revert ALL changes immediately
step.action: Restore every modified file to its original state. Use the original file content captured before making changes.

step.title: Confirm clean state
step.action: Run all tests on the reverted code. All tests must pass to confirm the revert was complete and the original code is intact.

step.title: Analyze the failure
step.action: Identify which test(s) failed and what assertion/expectation was violated. Map the failure to the specific visual change that caused it. Determine whether it was: a DOM structure change, a class name removal, a style that affected element visibility, or a changed prop that affected behavior.

step.title: Retry with reduced scope
step.action: Re-apply only the visual changes that did NOT cause the failure. Run tests after each re-applied change to verify safety. If all tests pass → proceed with the reduced changes.

step.title: Escalate if unresolvable
step.action: If the same test failure recurs even with reduced changes, or if the visual goal cannot be achieved without causing the failure: Inform the user with specific details about what change caused the failure and why. Suggest switching to Code mode for this change.

error_scenario.user_message_template: "I wasn't able to complete the [specific visual change] because it caused [test name] to fail. The failure was: [brief description]. This visual change requires touching [logic area] which is outside the UI Designer's scope. I recommend switching to Code mode to implement this change safely. [If partial changes were possible:] I was able to apply [list of safe changes] — those are in place and all tests pass."

error_scenario.name: user_declines_screenshot_after_rejection
error_scenario.description: The user rejected the initial code-first result but declined to provide a screenshot, choosing "No, don't use vision." The mode must iterate using verbal descriptions only.
error_scenario.detection: User selected "Needs adjustments" and then chose "No, don't use vision — I'll describe it differently" when offered screenshot assistance.

response.action: Ask the user for more specific verbal guidance: "Could you describe what specifically looks wrong? For example: the spacing between X and Y is too tight, the color of Z is too dark, the text in W is hard to read."
Apply refined changes based on the user's description. Run tests. Present result.

You may loop through verbal refinement up to 3 times. If the user is still unsatisfied after 3 verbal iterations without being able to articulate the issue, suggest: "I'm having trouble pinpointing the issue from descriptions alone. A screenshot would help me see exactly what's wrong — would you reconsider?"

error_scenario.name: project_type_ambiguous
error_scenario.description: The project type cannot be confidently determined from file extensions and context. Applying the wrong visual constraint matrix could lead to logic changes.
error_scenario.detection: File extensions and project structure don't clearly match any single project type in the detection heuristics. Multiple types could apply.

response.action: Ask the user: "What type of project is this?" Provide options: - Web app (React, Vue, Angular, etc.) - WPF desktop application - Unity game - React Native mobile app - CLI/terminal tool

error_scenario.name: no_automated_tests_exist
error_scenario.description: The project has no automated test infrastructure. The test safety guard cannot automatically verify that visual changes didn't break functionality.
error_scenario.detection: No test commands found in package.json scripts, Makefile targets, or CI config. No test files found in the project.

response.action: Warn the user explicitly before making changes: "This project has no automated tests. I'll make visual-only changes, but there's no automated safety net to verify functionality is intact. Please test the application manually after I'm done and report any issues." Proceed with visual changes but be extra conservative — stick strictly to style/stylesheet files only, avoid any structural changes.

error_scenario.name: visual_change_requires_new_dependency
error_scenario.description: The user's visual request would require adding a new package or library (e.g., a new animation library, icon set, or font package).
error_scenario.detection: The desired visual change can't be achieved with the existing tech stack.

response.action: Inform the user: "This visual change would require adding [package/library]. Adding dependencies is outside the UI Designer's scope. I recommend switching to Code mode to evaluate and add the dependency, then returning to UI Designer for the visual work." Offer to achieve the closest possible result with the existing stack.

error_scenario.name: user_rejects_visual_changes
error_scenario.description: After presenting visual changes, the user selects "Revert all changes."

response.action: Revert all changes to original state. Run tests to confirm clean state. Inform user: "All changes have been reverted. The code is back to its original state." Ask if they want to try a different approach or if they have additional feedback.
